evidence_excerpt: Mouth opening is now normal... has had no further difficulties with either feeding or airway.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: No further difficulties with either feeding or airway.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: At review 8 weeks later, the infant had a 30-mm interalveolar opening.
Tongue base advancement with a circummandibular Prolene suture was performed at 8 months of age.
In the following weeks oral intake increased to the extent that nasogastric feeding was able to be ceased.
Underwent successful elective decannulation of his tracheostomy at 12 months of age.
The patient had an uncomplicated cleft palate repair at 18 months of age.
Has had no further difficulties with either feeding or airway.

notes_on_uncertainty: The article reports two different cases. This summary pertains to Case 1 (VWS with interalveolar synechiae). Exact diagnosis date of the synechiae is not specified; events are aligned approximately relative to when the adhesions were identified and managed.
